market_context: US stock futures are rising as of late March 2026, with oil prices climbing amid a shortened trading week featuring jobs data and war uncertainty, following gains in S&P 500 (0.9%), Dow (0.9%), and Nasdaq (0.8%). February saw market volatility with Nasdaq down 3.4% due to AI fears, stretched tech valuations, and tariff concerns, while S&P 500 fell 0.8% and Dow was flat 0.3%; Q4 2025 GDP slowed to 1.4% with persistent core PCE at 3.0%.[1][2]

predictions.reasoning: Futures are rising per recent coverage, extending Monday's 0.9% S&P 500 gain amid jobs data anticipation.[1] February's -0.8% dip reflected AI and tariff worries, but cyclical resilience and slowing growth without contraction suggest rebound potential.[2] Pre-market momentum supports mild upside despite uncertainty.

Nasdaq fell 3.4% in February on AI fears rattling tech valuations and SaaS disruption concerns.[2][3] Recent 0.8% bounce may fade with persistent core PCE at 3.0% delaying rate cuts and tariff headlines pressuring multinationals.[1][2] Tech-heavy exposure heightens volatility risk today.

Dow held flat at 0.3% in February with resilience in energy, materials, and industrials amid tech selloff.[2] Monday's 0.9% rise and rising futures indicate strength in value cyclicals over growth.[1] Stable labor data and Q4 GDP moderation favor industrials end-of-day.

Oil prices are climbing alongside rising futures, boosting energy sector resilience noted in February.[1][2] Cyclical segments like energy outperformed tech amid growth moderation and inflation persistence.[2] Pre-market oil strength positions XLE for gains.
